title: Departments With a High Earner
stage: Stage 5 • Test whether a related row exists
learning_objective: Use correlated EXISTS to keep each department only when at least one related employee meets a condition.
why: EXISTS expresses “keep this parent row when at least one matching child row meets the condition.”
prompt: Return department_id and department_name for departments that have at least one employee with salary greater than or equal to 95000. Sort by department_id.
build_steps: Use departments as the outer table and employees as the inner search table.
Correlate employees to the current department using `department_id`.
Add the salary threshold inside the related-row search.
Use EXISTS to keep the department when at least one matching employee is found.
explanation: The outer query reads departments. The inner query searches employees for a matching department and qualifying salary.
common_mistakes: Putting the employee salary condition in the outer department query.
Forgetting to connect employee rows to the current department.
Using COUNT when a simple existence test communicates the intent more clearly.
hints: EXISTS stops being about returned columns; it only needs at least one matching row.
The department ID condition correlates the two queries.
takeaways: EXISTS is ideal for “at least one related record.”
reflection_questions: Does EXISTS care whether the inner query selects 1, employee_id, or another column?
What two conditions must be true for an employee row to satisfy the inner search?
stretch_goal: Change the threshold to 90,000 and predict which additional departments will appear.
starter_sql:
SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name
FROM departments AS d
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM employees AS e
    WHERE e.department_id = d.department_id
      -- add the salary threshold
)
ORDER BY d.department_id;
solution_explanation: The inner query is a related-row search, not a value-producing calculation. EXISTS keeps an outer department as soon as at least one employee from that department meets the salary threshold.
solution_walkthrough: The department ID condition correlates the inner employee search with the current outer department.
The salary condition defines the qualifying related row.
EXISTS converts “one or more matches” into a yes-or-no filter.
subtitle: Stage 5 • Test whether a related row exists
